data.name: Apex - Good Catch Report - New
data.description: Health and Safety Form for all Apex Users

entries.label:
At Apex we use Good Catches as a means to pro-actively report near hits/misses, unsafe conditions, unsafe acts, safe behaviors, and safety observations. Good Catch reports are leading indicators for our WorkSafe program, and are used in part to evaluate the effectiveness of the program, as well as to determine training needs and potential changes to the program. In other words, this report is a very valuable learning tool. It is important to remember our "No Name, No Blame" policy for reports of unsafe acts and/or conditions. We do not need to know who, only that we identified potential hazards and corrected them before they became an incident.

A corrective action must be included with all Good Catch reports. Without a practical corrective action, the situation may not be a Good Catch. When in doubt, ask! Ask colleagues, supervisors, managers, or the Apex Director of H&S.
IF SOMEONE WAS INJURED OR PROPERTY DAMAGE OCCURRED. DO NOT USE THIS FORM!
Worker accidents resulting in injury or property damage require completion of an Apex Incident Report
Apex Office Location of person identifying the Good Catch:
*** Please select "CSW" if the person identifying the Good Catch is in the Commercial Stormwater Division ***
Division of person identifying the Good Catch:
Describe the Good Catch - Remember, it must have a practical corrective action:
What corrective action was implemented to prevent the Good Catch from becoming an Incident?
Who is responsible for implementing the corrective action?
What are the lessons learned from the identification and correction of the Good Catch?
